# papers Documentation

> Static documentation template

A minimal docs starter with install-ready defaults, generated content, and static-host deployment support.

## Getting Started

- [Introduction](/docs/getting-started/introduction): `papers` is a static docs template for teams that want a polished documentation site without adding a CMS or backend first.
- [Quick Start](/docs/getting-started/quick-start): Use this path if you want your own docs site running quickly with the least amount of ceremony.
- [Installation](/docs/getting-started/installation): - Node.js 18 or newer

## User Guide

- [Basic Usage](/docs/user-guide/basic-usage): The day-to-day workflow is simple: edit content, regenerate docs output, preview locally, then ship a static build.
- [Advanced Features](/docs/user-guide/advanced-features): `papers` ships with more than plain Markdown rendering.
- [Configuration](/docs/user-guide/configuration): Most customization happens in a small set of files.
- [Troubleshooting](/docs/user-guide/troubleshooting): The app reads generated JSON, not raw Markdown directly.
- [Command Palette](/docs/user-guide/command-palette): The command palette is the fastest way to move through the site.
- [Interactive Map](/docs/user-guide/interactive-map): The interactive map renders the documentation tree as a graph in the right rail.

## API Reference

- [Overview](/docs/api-reference/overview): This section covers the internal runtime and build APIs that power the template.
- [Content Pipeline](/docs/api-reference/content-pipeline): The content pipeline turns Markdown source files into static artifacts the app can load directly.
- [Runtime APIs](/docs/api-reference/runtime-apis): Fetches and caches `/docs-index.json` for the current session.

## Developer Guides

- [Code Examples](/docs/developer-guides/code-examples): This page shows the main authoring patterns supported by the current runtime.
- [Best Practices](/docs/developer-guides/best-practices): Treat `shared/documentation-config.js` as the source of truth for navigation.
- [Design System](/docs/developer-guides/design-system): The current visual system is intentionally minimal: monochrome, high-contrast, and token-driven.
- [UI Configuration](/docs/developer-guides/ui-configuration): The docs shell is mostly configured through a few focused files.
- [Icon Customization](/docs/developer-guides/icon-customization): The app uses Iconify for most UI icons and a small amount of custom SVG usage when you want full control.

## Platform Guides

- [Cloudflare](/docs/deployment/platforms/cloudflare): Use these values in Cloudflare Pages:
- [Vercel](/docs/deployment/platforms/vercel): When importing the repo, use:
- [Netlify](/docs/deployment/platforms/netlify): Use these values in Netlify:

## Deployment

- [Overview](/docs/deployment/overview): `papers` builds to a static site with route-specific HTML output.
- [Production Setup](/docs/deployment/production-setup): Before you ship, set the values users and crawlers should actually see. You can provide them through `.env.local`, `.env.production`, or your host's build environment.
